fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Mexico Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to New Mexico - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity new mexico, how to transfer pharmacist license to new mexico, pharmacist compact new mexico, new mexico pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: New Mexico Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: New Mexico Regulation and Licensing Department
board.mailing_address: New Mexico Board of Pharmacy, P.O. Box 25101, Santa Fe, NM 87504

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in New Mexico.

steps.title: Create an Account in the RLD Online Licensing System
steps.description: Navigate to the New Mexico Regulation and Licensing Department's online portal at rld.state.nm.us/login/ and create a new user account.

steps.title: Complete the Online Application
steps.description: Log in to the RLD Online Licensing System, select the appropriate 'Pharmacist Endorsement' or 'Pharmacist by Examination' application, and complete all required sections, providing personal, educational, and professional history.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee (e.g., $150 for endorsement) through the RLD Online Licensing System using a credit card or electronic check.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload necessary documents directly to the RLD Online Licensing System as prompted. Arrange for official transcripts from your pharmacy school to be sent directly to the NM Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Request License Transfer & Score Reports (for Endorsement)
steps.description: For endorsement applicants, arrange for a verified record of your current license(s) and any relevant NABP score reports (NAPLEX/MPJE) to be sent directly to the Board of Pharmacy through NABP's Electronic Licensure Transfer Program (e-LEC) or from the issuing state board.

steps.title: Undergo Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided within the RLD Online Licensing System or on the Board's website to complete a fingerprint-based criminal background check.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a New Mexico pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does New Mexico have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in New Mexico. To work in New Mexico, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to New Mexico?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Mexico while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in New Mexico
hero.dek: Pharmacists seeking licensure in New Mexico must apply through the RLD Online Licensing System.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity

official_links.registration_steps: Click 'Create An Account' on the login page, then follow the prompts to provide personal information and establish your username and password.
